what: Add use_non_temporal_load=True to bs=16/E=257 cktile block_m=32 on top of the v176 sparse-E257 bundle
why: v176 became the strongest benchmarked candidate by combining bs128/E257 NT=True with bs512/E257 FlyDSL t16x256x128. The remaining adjacent sparse-path knob is bs16/E257: current submission already widens its cktile block to 32, but unlike bs128 and bs512 it still leaves load policy implicit. Sparse tiny-batch E257 has minimal reuse, so explicit NT may cut cache pollution without perturbing the stronger bs128/bs512 paths.

what: Change the v180 sparse-E257 bundle so bs=512/E=257 uses block_m=64 with FlyDSL t16x256x128 stage2
why: v176 materially improved benchmark by fixing bs128/E257 while preserving the bs512/E257 widened stage2. The remaining dominant hotspot is still bs512/E257. Earlier block_m=64 exploration for bs512/E257 was done before the later sparse-bundle improvements and without the stronger t16x256x128 plus bs128 NT combination, and at least one run timed out rather than disproving the config. This isolates whether coarser sorting granularity now combines better with the current best sparse bundle.

what: Apply bs=512/E=257 block_m=64 to the v176 sparse-E257 bundle without the bs16 NT regression
why: v180 benchmarked at 121.99us, worse than the stronger v176 baseline at 121.17us, so explicit NT on bs16/E257 is a losing change. The remaining useful isolation is still bs512/E257 sorting granularity. This retries block_m=64 only on top of the clean v176 bundle so the result is not confounded by the regressed bs16 path.

what: Disable non-temporal loads only for bs=512/E=257 on top of the v176 sparse-E257 bundle
why: The old v173 benchmark, although confounded by an older weaker bundle, showed the bs512/E257 shape itself improved when NT was disabled. v176 separately proved that bs128/E257 benefits from NT=True. This clean variant keeps the stronger v176 bs128 path and tests whether bs512 wants the opposite load policy once isolated from the older regressions.

what: Use block_m=64 for bs=128/E=257 on top of the clean v176 sparse-E257 bundle
why: v181 shows that block_m=64 on bs512/E257 is unstable, so the remaining safe sorting-granularity branch is bs128/E257. v176 established that this shape benefits from the cktile block_m=32 plus NT=True path; moving to block_m=64 keeps the stronger bundle intact while testing whether slightly coarser grouping reduces sorting overhead without triggering the bs512 crash mode.

what: Use ksplit=1 for bs=128/E=257 on top of the clean v176 sparse-E257 bundle
why: v183 shows bs512 NT=False is still worse than the clean v176 baseline, and v181/v182 killed the bs512 block_m=64 branch. The remaining safe hotspot is bs128/E257. v176 uses block_m=32 plus NT=True there, but still keeps ksplit=2 inherited from older sparse tuning. On the stronger bundle, the extra split-K reduction may now cost more than it helps; testing ksplit=1 isolates that overhead without touching the known-good bs512 stage2 choice.
